content.problemStatement:
Chef has a rooted tree with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN); vertex 111 is the root of the tree. Initially, there are some bacteria in its vertices. Let's denote the number of sons of vertex vvv by svs_vsv​; a leaf is a vertex without sons. During each second, the following events happen:


For each non-leaf vertex vvv, if there are xxx bacteria in this vertex at the start of this second, they divide into sv⋅xs_v \cdot xsv​⋅x bacteria. At the end of this second, xxx of these bacteria instantly move to each of its sons (none of them stay in vertex vvv). The original xxx bacteria do not exist any more.
Zero or more bacteria appear in one vertex. These bacteria do not divide or move at this second.
Initially, we are at the start of second 000. You should process QQQ queries ― one query during each of the seconds 000 through Q−1Q-1Q−1. There are two types of queries:


`+ v k`: During this second, kkk bacteria appear in vertex vvv.
`? v`: Find the number of bacteria in vertex vvv at the end of this second ― after the divided bacteria have moved.
constraints: the graph described on the input is a tree
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
Each of the next N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers xxx and yyy denoting that vertices xxx and yyy are connected by an edge.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,…,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1​,a2​,…,aN​ denoting the initial numbers of bacteria in vertices 111 through NNN.
QQQ lines follow. Each of these lines describes a query in the format `+ v k` or `? v`.
content.outputFormat: For each query of the second type, print a single line containing one integer ― the number of bacteria in the given vertex.
